title: Not Every Rubric Teaches Equally: Policy-Aware Rubric Rewards for RLVR
core_claim: POW3R is a policy-aware rubric reward framework for RLVR that preserves the human rubric objective while adapting criterion-level weights during training to emphasize criteria that currently distinguish rollouts, improving rubric reward and strict completion over vanilla GRPO in the authors' experiments.
triage_rationale: This looks like a solid methods paper with a clear training-side contribution and measurable claims across multiple policies and datasets, but the abstract suggests an incremental optimization improvement rather than a fundamentally new problem setting. It is worth review because rubric-based reward design and policy-aware weighting could be practically useful and easy to discuss, even if the evidence is still limited to the authors' benchmark setup.
likely_red_flags: Single-paper abstract claims only; no independent validation visible in triage
Benchmark-specific gains may not generalize beyond the reported GRPO/rubric setting
Method sounds like a training heuristic, so novelty may be modest
